input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows.

---

## Phase 1: .NET SDK

```powershell
# Install the latest .NET SDK via winget (discover available versions first)
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK
# Install latest .NET 9 SDK (MAUI's current target)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9 --silent

# Verify
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## Phase 2: MAUI Workload

```powershell
# See what workloads are available
dotnet workload search

# Install MAUI (pulls in android, ios, maccatalyst dependencies automatically)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Phase 3: Android SDK & JDK

The MAUI workload installs Android SDK bindings, but you need the actual Android SDK + a JDK.

```powershell
# Install OpenJDK 17 (required by Android tooling)
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17 --silent

# Set JAVA_HOME (winget installer usually does this; verify)
[System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('JAVA_HOME', 'Machine')

# If missing, set it:
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable(
  'JAVA_HOME',
  'C:\Program Files\Microsoft\jdk-17',  # adjust if path differs
  'Machine'
)
```

**Android SDK** — two options:

### Option A: Let Visual Studio manage it (recommended)
```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community --silent `
  --override "--add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat --passive"
# This installs VS + the .NET MAUI workload + Android SDK automatically
```

### Option B: Standalone (CLI-only, no VS)
```powershell
# Install Android command-line tools
winget install Google.AndroidCommandLineTools --silent

# Discover the ANDROID_HOME path
$androidHome = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable('ANDROID_HOME', $androidHome, 'User')
$env:ANDROID_HOME = $androidHome

# Accept licenses
& "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# Discover available platforms and build-tools dynamically
& "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
  Select-String 'platforms;android-|build-tools;'

# Install the latest stable API level + build tools (adjust numbers from output)
& "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-35" `
  "build-tools;35.0.0" `
  "platform-tools" `
  "emulator" `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"
```

## Phase 4: Android Emulator (optional)

```powershell
# Create an AVD
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" `
  create avd -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
  --device "pixel_6"

# Launch it
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_MAUI
```

> **Prerequisite:** Enable Hyper-V / WHPX for emulator acceleration:
> ```powershell
> # Run as Admin
> Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All -NoRestart
> ```

## Phase 5: Verify Everything

```powershell
# Create a test project
dotnet new maui -n MauiTest
cd MauiTest

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Deploy to Android emulator (with emulator running)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Install -t:Run
```

## Phase 6: Dynamic Version Discovery Cheat Sheet

| What                   | Command                                                      |
|------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| .NET SDKs available    | `winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK`                         |
| Installed SDKs         | `dotnet --list-sdks`                                         |
| Available workloads    | `dotnet workload search`                                     |
| Installed workloads    | `dotnet workload list`                                       |
| Android API levels     | `sdkmanager.bat --list \| Select-String 'platforms;android-'` |
| MAUI target frameworks | `dotnet new maui --dry-run` then inspect the `.csproj`       |
| Windows TFM versions   | `dir "C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Kits\10\Include"`       |

---

**TL;DR path:** If you want the least friction, install Visual Studio Community with the **.NET Multi-platform App UI** workload (Option A in Phase 3) — it handles the SDK, JDK, Android SDK, and emulator setup automatically. The CLI-only path works but requires more manual wiring.
